name: AJR
description: is an American pop band founded in 2005 by brothers {{nowrap|[[Adam Met|Adam]], Jack, and Ryan}} Met ({{Ne|Metzger}}), collectively a trio of vocalists, {{nowrap|multi-instrumentalists}}, and songwriters. AJR spent their early years [[busking]], [[tap dancing]] and singing covers around New York City, and emerged from songwriting at their home studio, where they recorded and produced the album, ''[[Living Room (AJR album)|Living Room]]'' (2015). In the 2010s and the 2020s, AJR has recorded [[List of songs recorded by AJR|over 100 songs]] and a live album and completed seven concert tours. Four of the headlining tours were separately supported by the studio albums: ''[[The Click (album)|The Click]]'' (2017); ''[[The Maybe Man]]'' (2023); the two [[Billboard 200|US ''Billboard'' 200]] top-ten albums ''[[Neotheater]]'' (2019) and ''[[OK Orchestra]]'' (2021). Music magazine [[Billboard (magazine)|''Billboard'']] ranked AJR as one of the 100 top-selling touring artists of 2024. The 2015 [[indie pop]] debut album yielded their first pop radio platinum single "[[I'm Ready (AJR song)|I'm Ready]]", the 2017 [[electropop]] platinum album contained their first [[List of Billboard Alternative Songs number ones of the 2010s|''Billboard'' Alternative number-one song]] "[[Sober Up]]" (featuring [[Rivers Cuomo]]), and the 2021 [[orchestral pop]] gold album spawned—their first [[List of Billboard Hot 100 top-ten singles in 2021|Hot 100 top-ten single]] and also the [[Billboard Music Award for Top Rock Song#Bang!|''Billboard'' Music Award Top Rock Song]]—"[[Bang! (AJR song)|Bang!]]". AJR is a {{nowrap|[[multi-platinum]]}} band for having eight platinum singles—including the three aforementioned songs; "[[Weak (AJR song)|Weak]]"; "[[Burn the House Down (song)|Burn the House Down]]"; "[[100 Bad Days]]"; "[[Way Less Sad]]"; and "[[World's Smallest Violin]]"—all these songs accounted for nineteen million [[Recording Industry Association of America#"Digital" single certification|certified units]] of their digital single sales in America.
